Architecture Fundamentals.aiRubric: Check concepts of monoliths vs microservices, scaling logic, load balancer config, caching, and database selection.

lessons.title: Monoliths vs. Microservices Architectural Patterns
lessons.theory:
## Architectural Patterns
Monoliths bundle all logic into one deployable unit. Microservices break them apart.
```text
Monolith -> [ UI + Auth + DB Logic ] -> DB
Microservices -> [ UI ] -> [ Auth API ] & [ DB API ]
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Architecture Concept
Assign the string `'Monolith'` to a variable representing a single-unit application.

lessons.title: Vertical vs. Horizontal Scaling Basics
lessons.theory:
## Scaling
Vertical: upgrade CPU/RAM. Horizontal: add more servers.
```text
Vertical: Server(2GB) -> Server(8GB)
Horizontal: Server -> Server + Server + Server
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Scaling Type
Assign `'Horizontal'` to the variable describing adding more server nodes.

lessons.title: Load Balancers & Reverse Proxies (Nginx)
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Nginx Upstream
Define an Nginx upstream block named `app_servers` with one server `192.168.1.10`.

lessons.title: Caching Strategies (Client, CDN, Server side)
lessons.theory:
## Caching
Caching temporarily stores copies of data to serve it faster.
```json
{
  "Cache-Control": "max-age=3600"
}
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Cache Header
Set a JSON header configuration with `Cache-Control` set to `'max-age=86400'`.

lessons.title: Database Selection: SQL vs. NoSQL Architectural Choices
lessons.theory:
## DB Choices
SQL is relational (ACID). NoSQL is non-relational (flexible schema, BASE).
```text
SQL: Users Table -> Rows/Columns
NoSQL: Users Collection -> JSON Documents
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: DB Type
Assign `'NoSQL'` to the variable describing a document-based database.

lessons.title: Core Concepts of Architecture Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Understanding Architecture Fundamentals\nBefore diving into complex implementations, it's essential to understand the foundational principles of Architecture Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Define Core Concept\n1. Create a function `get_concept()` that returns the string 'Architecture Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.hint: Return the exact string 'Architecture Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.rubric: Function returns the correct concept string.

lessons.title: Setting up the Environment for Architecture Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Tooling\nProperly configuring your development environment is the first step in mastering Architecture Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment Status\n1. Create a function `is_ready()` that returns True.

lessons.title: Basic Syntax and Operations
lessons.theory: ## Syntax Rules\nEvery discipline has its syntax. Here we apply standard operations relevant to Architecture Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Basic Math\n1. Return the sum of a and b.
lessons.starterCode: def add(a, b):\n    return a ___ b
lessons.solution: def add(a, b):\n    return a + b

lessons.title: Control Structures in Architecture Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Branching\nConditional logic dictates how applications respond to different states.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Check State\n1. Return 'Active' if state is 1, else 'Inactive'.

lessons.title: Advanced Memory Management
lessons.theory: ## Resource Allocation\nEfficient memory use is critical, especially when applying Architecture Fundamentals at scale.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clear Memory\n1. Set the variable `cache` to an empty dictionary.

lessons.title: Object-Oriented Approaches
lessons.theory: ## OOP\nEncapsulating state and behavior within objects helps structure Architecture Fundamentals projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Create Class\n1. Create a class `Module` with a property `name` initialized to 'Architecture Fundamentals'.

lessons.title: Functional Programming Paradigms
lessons.theory: ## Pure Functions\nFunctional programming avoids side effects. This is increasingly popular in Architecture Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pure Function\n1. Write a function `square(x)` that returns x squared.

lessons.title: Error Handling and Debugging
lessons.theory: ## Resilience\nHandling exceptions gracefully ensures robustness in Architecture Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Try/Except\n1. Wrap the division in a try block, catch ZeroDivisionError, and return None.

lessons.title: Advanced Debugging in Architecture Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Debugging at Scale\nFinding hard-to-reproduce bugs requires structured methodology.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Debug Setup\n1. Return True for debugging active.

lessons.title: Profiling Architecture Fundamentals Applications
lessons.theory: ## Performance Profiling\nIdentifying bottlenecks is the first step in optimization.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Profile Scope\n1. Set `profile_mode` to 'High'.

lessons.title: Memory Leaks and Management
lessons.theory: ## Garbage Collection\nUnderstanding how memory is freed is critical.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: GC Status\n1. Set `gc_enabled` to True.

lessons.title: Multi-threading Constructs
lessons.theory: ## Parallelism\nUtilizing multiple cores improves execution speed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Thread Count\n1. Set `threads` to 8.

lessons.title: Final Project: Architecture Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Capstone\nApply all learned concepts to build a comprehensive solution.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Project Initialization\n1. Set `project_name` to 'Architecture Fundamentals Final'.

Distributed Systems.aiRubric: Check understanding of CAP theorem, Redis caching, message queues, replication, and sharding.

lessons.title: Architectural Trade-offs: The CAP Theorem
lessons.theory:
## CAP Theorem
Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance. Pick two.
```text
CA: RDBMS
CP: MongoDB
AP: Cassandra
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: CAP Choice
Assign `'Availability'` to the missing property if a system prioritizes `Partition Tolerance` and staying online.

lessons.title: Distributed Caching with Redis & Memcached
lessons.theory:
## Redis
In-memory key-value data store.
```bash
SET user:1 "Ada"
GET user:1
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Redis CLI
Write the Redis command to set the key `session_1` to `active`.

lessons.title: Message Queues & Event-Driven Architecture (RabbitMQ/Kafka)
lessons.theory:
## Message Queues
Decouple services using async messaging.
```text
Producer -> [ Queue ] -> Consumer
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Architecture Flow
Assign `'Producer'` to the service sending messages, and `'Consumer'` to the service receiving.

lessons.title: Data Replication & Master-Slave Architectures
lessons.theory:
## Replication
Copying data across multiple servers. Master handles Writes, Slaves handle Reads.
```text
Write -> Master -> (Sync) -> Slave 1, Slave 2
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Read/Write Split
Assign `'Writes'` to Master tasks, and `'Reads'` to Slave tasks.
lessons.rubric: Variables correctly map Writes and Reads.

lessons.title: Distributed ID Generation & Sharding Keys
lessons.theory:
## Sharding
Partitioning data across multiple databases. Requires a good Shard Key.
```text
ID -> Hash(ID) % N -> Shard Server
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Hashing Concept
Assign `'Modulo'` to the operator commonly used to distribute IDs across `N` servers.

lessons.title: Concurrency and Async Operations
lessons.theory: ## Asynchronous Work\nBlocking operations freeze applications. Async allows overlapping tasks in Distributed Systems.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Async Function\n1. Define an async function `fetch_data()`.

lessons.title: Working with Data Structures
lessons.theory: ## Data Organization\nChoosing the right data structure dictates performance.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Use a Set\n1. Return a set containing the elements 1, 2, 3.

lessons.title: Network Communication Protocols
lessons.theory: ## Connectivity\nUnderstanding HTTP/TCP is essential for networked components in Distributed Systems.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: HTTP Status\n1. Return 200 for OK.

lessons.title: File I/O and Storage
lessons.theory: ## Persistence\nSaving data to disk allows state to survive reboots.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Open File\n1. Write code to open 'data.txt' in read mode.

lessons.title: Security Best Practices
lessons.theory: ## Defense\nProtecting applications from vulnerabilities is non-negotiable.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Hash Length\n1. Return 64, the length of a SHA-256 hash in hex.

lessons.title: Performance Optimization Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Efficiency\nOptimizing critical paths improves throughput in Distributed Systems.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Time Complexity\n1. Return 'O(1)' for constant time complexity.

lessons.title: Unit Testing Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Validation\nUnit tests verify that individual components work as expected.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Assert True\n1. Write an assert statement that 1 equals 1.

lessons.title: Integration Testing
lessons.theory: ## System Harmony\nIntegration tests ensure that components function together.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Integration Status\n1. Set `integrated` to True.

High Availability & Scalability.aiRubric: Check concepts of fault tolerance, CDNs, consistent hashing, circuit breakers, and disaster recovery.

lessons.title: Designing for Fault Tolerance & Rate Limiting
lessons.theory:
## Rate Limiting
Controls traffic to prevent overload.
```bash
# Nginx limit_req_zone
limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=mylimit:10m rate=1r/s;
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: HTTP Status
What HTTP status code is commonly returned for Too Many Requests?

lessons.title: Content Delivery Networks (CDNs) & Edge Computing
lessons.theory:
## CDNs
Serve static assets from servers geographically closer to the user.
```text
User (Tokyo) -> CDN Node (Tokyo) -> Origin (NY)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: CDN Purpose
Assign `'Latency'` to the primary metric CDNs aim to reduce.

lessons.title: Database Sharding & Consistent Hashing
lessons.theory:
## Consistent Hashing
Minimizes data movement when nodes are added or removed.
```text
Hash Ring [ Node A, Node B, Node C ]
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Rebalancing
Does consistent hashing require full data rebalancing? Assign `false`.

lessons.title: Microservice Patterns: Circuit Breakers & API Gateways
lessons.theory:
## Circuit Breakers
Prevent cascading failures when a downstream service is down.
```text
State: Closed -> Open -> Half-Open
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Circuit State
Assign `'Open'` to the state where the circuit breaker instantly rejects requests.

lessons.title: Disaster Recovery, Backups, and Multi-Region Deployment
lessons.theory:
## Disaster Recovery
RTO (Recovery Time Objective) and RPO (Recovery Point Objective) define backup SLAs.
```text
Region A (Active) <--> Region B (Standby)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: SLA Metrics
Assign `'RTO'` to the metric defining how fast a system must recover.

lessons.title: Continuous Integration Setup
lessons.theory: ## CI Pipelines\nCI automatically runs tests when code is pushed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pipeline Step\n1. Create a list with 'build', 'test', 'deploy'.

lessons.title: Deployment Pipelines
lessons.theory: ## Shipping Code\nDeploying reliably requires automated release pipelines.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment\n1. Set `env` to 'production'.

lessons.title: Logging and Monitoring
lessons.theory: ## Observability\nLogs help diagnose issues in production.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Log Level\n1. Set `level` to 'ERROR'.

lessons.title: Design Patterns in High Availability & Scalability
lessons.theory: ## Reusable Solutions\nDesign patterns solve common architectural problems.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Singleton\n1. Set `pattern` to 'Singleton'.

lessons.title: API Integration Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Connecting Services\nAPIs allow different systems to communicate.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: JSON Data\n1. Set `format` to 'JSON'.

lessons.title: Database Connectivity
lessons.theory: ## Data Access\nConnecting to a DB safely is essential.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Connection String\n1. Set `conn_str` to 'postgres://localhost'.

lessons.title: Scaling Applications
lessons.theory: ## Horizontal Scaling\nAdding more instances handles increased load.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Instance Count\n1. Set `instances` to 3.

lessons.title: Microservices Architecture
lessons.theory: ## Decoupling\nMicroservices break large apps into independent services.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Service Name\n1. Set `service` to 'Auth'.

lessons.title: Best Practices and Anti-patterns
lessons.theory: ## Writing Clean Code\nMaintainability ensures the long-term success of High Availability & Scalability projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clean Code\n1. Set `is_clean` to True.
